display.purpose: Hero title and arcade cabinet moments only.

headline.purpose: Major in-world sections such as Medal Cabinet and Builder's Manual.

label.purpose: Short arcade labels, stat captions, and nav labels.

shadows.name: cabinet-depth
shadows.purpose: Embedded play surface and major cabinet panel only.

motion.name: reveal-duration
motion.purpose: Section reveal when motion is allowed.

components.name: Primary Arcade Button
components.description: Primary play/start action with tactile arcade feedback.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #ffd166; color: #07131f; border: 2px solid #07131f; border-radius: 4px; padding: 1rem 2.5rem; font-family: Nunito, system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 900; letter-spacing: 0.12em; text-transform: uppercase; box-shadow: 0 4px 0 #07131f; cursor: pointer; transition: transform 120ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1), box-shadow 120ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1), background-color 160ms; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { transform: translateY(-2px); box-shadow: 0 6px 0 #07131f; } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: translateY(3px); box-shadow: 0 1px 0 #07131f; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 4px 0 #07131f, 0 0 0 4px rgba(255, 209, 102, 0.28); }

components.name: Speed HUD Chip
components.description: Compact game-state panel for multiplier, pipe count, or medal threshold.
components.css: .ds-hud-chip { display: inline-flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 0.35rem; align-items: center; background: #0d1e3a; color: #e8f8ff; border: 2px solid #2d6a4f; border-radius: 4px; padding: 0.75rem 1rem; box-shadow: 0 3px 0 #07131f; } .ds-hud-value { color: #ff6b35; font-family: Nunito, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 2rem; font-weight: 900; line-height: 1; } .ds-hud-label { color: #6ba8c8; font-family: Nunito, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.72rem; font-weight: 800; letter-spacing: 0.12em; text-transform: uppercase; }

components.name: Arcade Panel Card
components.description: Structured content panel for rules, docs, or build notes without generic glass styling.
components.html:
Builder's Manual

Game Engine

Physics, collision, scoring, and the game state machine.
components.css: .ds-panel-card { background: #0a1e3c; color: #e8f8ff; border: 2px solid rgba(74, 158, 111, 0.75); border-radius: 16px; padding: 1.5rem; box-shadow: 0 4px 0 #07131f; max-width: 22rem; } .ds-panel-card h3 { margin: 0 0 0.6rem; font-family: Nunito, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 1.25rem; font-weight: 900; line-height: 1.2; } .ds-panel-card p { margin: 0; color: #6ba8c8; font-family: Space Grotesk, system-ui, sans-serif; line-height: 1.55; } .ds-panel-kicker { color: #ffd166 !important; font-family: Nunito, system-ui, sans-serif !important; font-size: 0.72rem !important; font-weight: 900 !important; letter-spacing: 0.12em !important; text-transform: uppercase; margin-bottom: 0.5rem !important; }

components.name: HUD Navigation
components.description: Short game-world navigation that reads as a cabinet HUD instead of glassmorphism.
components.html: PlayRulesDocsGitHub
components.css: .ds-hud-nav { display: inline-flex; gap: 0.25rem; background: #07131f; border: 2px solid #2d6a4f; border-radius: 4px; padding: 0.35rem; box-shadow: 0 4px 0 rgba(0,0,0,0.25); } .ds-hud-nav a { color: #e8f8ff; text-decoration: none; font-family: Nunito, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.78rem; font-weight: 900; letter-spacing: 0.1em; text-transform: uppercase; padding: 0.65rem 0.9rem; border-radius: 3px; transition: background-color 160ms, color 160ms, transform 160ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1); } .ds-hud-nav a:hover, .ds-hud-nav a:focus-visible { background: #0d1e3a; color: #ffd166; outline: none; } .ds-hud-nav a.is-active { background: #ffd166; color: #07131f; }

components.name: Medal Cabinet Slot
components.description: Achievement-style medal slot for score thresholds and multiplier motivation.
components.html: Gold50 pipes1.50× danger zone
components.css: .ds-medal-slot { width: 10rem; min-height: 8rem; display: grid; place-items: center; text-align: center; gap: 0.35rem; background: linear-gradient(180deg, #0d1e3a, #0a1e3c); border: 2px solid #ffd166; border-radius: 4px; color: #e8f8ff; box-shadow: inset 0 0 0 3px rgba(255, 209, 102, 0.12), 0 4px 0 #07131f; padding: 1rem; } .ds-medal-name { color: #ffd166; font-family: Nunito, system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 900; font-size: 1.35rem; text-transform: uppercase; } .ds-medal-score { color: #e8f8ff; font-family: Space Grotesk, system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 700; } .ds-medal-note { color: #ff6b35; font-family: Nunito, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.72rem; font-weight: 900; letter-spacing: 0.08em; text-transform: uppercase; }

narrative.overview: Speedy Bird is a playable night-sky arcade cabinet, not a generic project page with game decoration. The system should feel like a familiar Flappy-style world sharpened around one escalating mechanic: every pipe makes the run faster. The page uses deep sky blues, pipe greens, arcade gold, and speed orange to stage a small but energetic game world.
keyCharacteristics: Deep night-sky surfaces with arcade gold and speed orange accents.
Pixel-inspired outlines, tactile buttons, HUD chips, scoreboards, and medal slots.
Play-first hierarchy: the run, multiplier, controls, and medals come before explanatory density.
Technical credibility framed as an in-world builder's manual.
Motion that suggests speed, but stops cleanly for reduced-motion users.

rules.name: The Gold Is the Button Rule
rules.body: Arcade Gold is reserved for action, achievement, and critical HUD state. Do not use it as generic decoration across every card.

rules.name: The Orange Means Speed Rule
rules.body: Speed Orange means acceleration, danger, or multiplier. If orange appears, it should teach the player something about intensity.

rules.name: The No Pure Fill Rule
rules.body: Pure black and pure white are allowed only as tiny sprite-like strokes when preserving pixel-art language. Large surfaces must use tinted night-sky tokens.

rules.name: The Scoreboard Number Rule
rules.body: Multipliers, scores, medal thresholds, and pipe counts must be visually stronger than their labels. The game is understood through numbers.

rules.name: The No Costume Mono Rule
rules.body: Do not use monospace as a lazy signal for technical credibility. Use it only for real code or command snippets in the builder manual.

rules.name: The No Ambient Glow Rule
rules.body: Glows must explain state: focus, active, selected, or danger. Decorative glow on dark surfaces is prohibited.

rules.name: The Pixel Layer Rule
rules.body: Prefer hard offsets, strokes, and stepped color ramps over blurred glass and floating cards.

dos: Do keep the night-sky, bird, pipe, medal, and arcade-gold identity intact.
Do make the speed multiplier visually central through HUD chips, timelines, and threshold markers.
Do use pipes, scoreboards, medals, and cabinet panels as structure, not decoration.
Do cap body copy at 65-75ch and let numbers carry the game mechanic.
Do add prefers-reduced-motion behavior for clouds, particles, CTA pulse, pipe sway, and reveal animations.
Do use focus-visible rings that are as intentional as hover states.
Do keep docs and GitHub links visible, but frame them as a builder's manual.
donts: Don't turn the page into a generic SaaS landing page wearing game colors.
Don't use gradient text for the hero title or major headings.
Don't use glassmorphism as the default surface treatment.
Don't rely on dark glows as the main source of visual energy.
Don't repeat identical feature-card grids when a game-native module would communicate better.
Don't use pure black or pure white as large fills; reserve them for tiny sprite strokes only.
Don't animate layout properties such as width, padding, or margin.
Don't hide primary play or contact actions behind novelty interactions.
Don't use monospace as developer cosplay; use it for real code only.
